name: Stuffed Baked Ham
recipeIngredient: 1 package Frozen chopped spinach, thawed, 10 oz
recipeInstructions:
1. 3/4 cup Packed watercress
2. 1/2 cup Packed celery leaves
3. 1/2 teaspoon Salt
4. 1/4 teaspoon Pepper
Glaze:
5. 1/2 cup Honey
6. 2 tablespoons Cider vinegar
7. 2 teaspoons Mustard
8. 2 teaspoons Dry ginger
9. Watercress for garnish (optional)
1. Remove the rind, if any, from the ham.
2. Trim away all but about 1/4 inch of fat from the ham.
3. Using a small sharp knife, make X-shaped cuts about 2 inches deep and 1 inch apart all over the fat side of the ham.
4. Preheat oven to 325°F.
5. Squeeze as much water as possible from the thawed kale and spinach.
6. Place the vegetables, onion, watercress, celery leaves, salt, and pepper in a food processor.
7. Pulse until finely chopped.
8. Press the vegetable mixture into the cavities cut into the ham, packing it well.
9. Place the ham fat side up in a shallow baking pan.
10. Bake for 2 hours.
11. Stir together honey, cider vinegar, mustard, and ginger in a small bowl.
12. Brush part of the glaze onto the ham.
13. Continue baking and basting for 30 minutes, until the ham is richly glazed.
14. Remove the ham from the oven and let it rest for 20 minutes before carving.
15. Carefully carve the ham, holding the slices to keep the stuffing in place.
16. Garnish platter with watercress if desired.
